---
title: "Score Computation"
subtitle: "Score per counterparty and variable contribution"
date: last-modified
sidebar: auto
number-sections: false
toc: true
author:
  - Jumbong Junior
format:
  html:
    mainfont: Times New Roman
    fontsize: 1.1em
jupyter: python3
---

```{python}
from pathlib import Path

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
from matplotlib.ticker import PercentFormatter

from config import data_path, data_output_path
from src.modeling.score_computation import (
    build_score_outputs,
    build_conditional_density_table,
    build_score_class_table,
    build_score_class_stability_tables,
    build_score_vingtile_default_rate,
    load_score_model_artifact,
)
```

## Load datasets and selected model

```{python}
train_dis = pd.read_csv(Path(data_path) / "train_discretized.csv")
test_dis = pd.read_csv(Path(data_path) / "test_discretized.csv")
oot_dis = pd.read_csv(Path(data_path) / "oot_discretized.csv")

model_pickle = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "best_4_variables_logit_scorecard.pkl"
)

score_artifact = load_score_model_artifact(model_pickle)
score_artifact["formula"]
```

## Compute modality scores and individual scores

The selected logistic model estimates default risk. Higher coefficients indicate higher default risk.  
The score is therefore defined so that low-risk modalities receive more points, and the total score ranges from 0 to 1000.

```{python}
modality_score_table, scored_datasets, variable_contributions = build_score_outputs(
    artifact=score_artifact,
    train_df=train_dis,
    test_df=test_dis,
    oot_df=oot_dis,
    score_scale=1000
)

modality_score_table
```

## Variable contributions

`scale_contribution_pct` measures the maximum possible contribution of each variable to the 0-1000 scale.  
`score_contribution_pct` measures the empirical contribution using the train distribution of modalities.

```{python}
variable_contributions
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))

plot_df = variable_contributions.set_index("variable_label")[
    ["scale_contribution_pct", "score_contribution_pct"]
]
plot_df.plot(kind="bar", ax=ax)

ax.axhline(
    50,
    color="red",
    linestyle="--",
    linewidth=1,
    label="50% threshold"
)
ax.set_title("Variable contributions")
ax.set_xlabel("")
ax.set_ylabel("Contribution (%)")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)
plt.xticks(rotation=30, ha="right")
plt.show()
```

## Score distribution

```{python}
score_distribution = pd.concat(
    [
        scored_datasets["Train"][["dataset", "score_total"]],
        scored_datasets["Test"][["dataset", "score_total"]],
        scored_datasets["OOT"][["dataset", "score_total"]],
    ],
    ignore_index=True
)

score_distribution.groupby("dataset")["score_total"].describe()
```

```{python}
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))

for dataset_name, dataset_scores in score_distribution.groupby("dataset"):
    ax.hist(
        dataset_scores["score_total"].dropna(),
        bins=30,
        alpha=0.45,
        label=dataset_name
    )

ax.set_title("Distribution of total score by dataset")
ax.set_xlabel("Total score")
ax.set_ylabel("Number of counterparties")
ax.legend()
ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)
plt.show()
```

## Conditional score density by default status

These charts compare the distribution of scores conditional on the observed default status.  
The more separated the two curves are, the more discriminating the score is. Since a high score means lower risk, non-default observations are expected to be shifted toward higher scores.

```{python}
conditional_density = build_conditional_density_table(
    scored_datasets=scored_datasets,
    target="def",
    score_col="score_total",
    grid_size=300,
    score_min=0,
    score_max=1000
)

conditional_density.head()
```

```{python}
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 4), sharey=True)

for ax, dataset_name in zip(axes, ["Train", "Test", "OOT"]):
    density_df = conditional_density.loc[
        conditional_density["dataset"].eq(dataset_name)
    ]

    for target_value, label, color in [
        (0, "Non-default", "#2F6B3F"),
        (1, "Default", "#B51E18"),
    ]:
        curve = density_df.loc[density_df["def"].eq(target_value)]
        ax.plot(
            curve["score"],
            curve["density"],
            label=label,
            color=color,
            linewidth=2
        )

    ax.set_title(dataset_name, fontweight="bold", fontsize=13)
    ax.text(
        0.02,
        0.94,
        dataset_name,
        transform=ax.transAxes,
        fontsize=12,
        fontweight="bold",
        verticalalignment="top",
        bbox={
            "boxstyle": "round,pad=0.25",
            "facecolor": "white",
            "edgecolor": "#444444",
            "alpha": 0.85,
        },
    )
    ax.set_xlabel("Score")
    ax.grid(alpha=0.3)

axes[0].set_ylabel("Density")
axes[-1].legend(loc="upper right")
fig.suptitle("Conditional score density by default status")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Final scored base

```{python}
base_final_with_score = pd.concat(
    [
        scored_datasets["Train"][["year", "def", "score_total", "dataset"]],
        scored_datasets["Test"][["year", "def", "score_total", "dataset"]],
        scored_datasets["OOT"][["year", "def", "score_total", "dataset"]],
    ],
    ignore_index=True
)

base_final_with_score = base_final_with_score[
    ["year", "def", "score_total", "dataset"]
]

base_final_with_score.groupby("dataset").agg(
    n=("score_total", "size"),
    min_score=("score_total", "min"),
    mean_score=("score_total", "mean"),
    max_score=("score_total", "max"),
    default_rate=("def", "mean")
)
```

## Default rate by score vingtile

The default rate is the proportion of loans in default among all loans in each score bucket.  
The score is split into 20 ordered buckets, from the lowest scores to the highest scores.

```{python}
default_rate_by_vingtile = build_score_vingtile_default_rate(
    scored_df=base_final_with_score,
    target="def",
    score_col="score_total",
    n_bins=20
)

default_rate_by_vingtile
```

```{python}
score_vingtile_plot_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "default_rate_by_score_vingtile.png"
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

ax.plot(
    default_rate_by_vingtile["vingtile"],
    default_rate_by_vingtile["default_rate"],
    color="#9D1712",
    marker="o",
    linewidth=2.2,
)
ax.bar(
    default_rate_by_vingtile["vingtile"],
    default_rate_by_vingtile["default_rate"],
    color="#D9D9D9",
    edgecolor="#8C8C8C",
    alpha=0.7,
)

ax.set_title("Default rate by score vingtile")
ax.set_xlabel("Score vingtile, from lowest score to highest score")
ax.set_ylabel("Default rate")
ax.set_xticks(default_rate_by_vingtile["vingtile"])
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(PercentFormatter(xmax=1, decimals=0))
ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)
ax.set_ylim(0, default_rate_by_vingtile["default_rate"].max() + 0.12)

score_class_boundaries = [3.5, 6.5, 8.5, 12.5, 17.5]
score_class_groups = [
    (1, 3, "Class 1\n[0 ; 241]"),
    (4, 6, "Class 2\n[241 ; 331]"),
    (7, 8, "Class 3\n[332 ; 498]"),
    (9, 12, "Class 4\n[498 ; 589]"),
    (13, 17, "Class 5\n[589 ; 780]"),
    (18, 20, "Class 6\n[781 ; 1000]"),
]

for boundary in score_class_boundaries:
    ax.axvline(
        boundary,
        color="#2F2F2F",
        linestyle="--",
        linewidth=1.2,
        alpha=0.7,
    )

for start, end, label in score_class_groups:
    ax.text(
        (start + end) / 2,
        0.97,
        label,
        transform=ax.get_xaxis_transform(),
        ha="center",
        va="top",
        fontsize=8,
        bbox={
            "boxstyle": "round,pad=0.25",
            "facecolor": "white",
            "edgecolor": "#B0B0B0",
            "alpha": 0.85,
        },
    )

for _, row in default_rate_by_vingtile.iterrows():
    ax.annotate(
        f"{row['default_rate']:.1%}",
        (row["vingtile"], row["default_rate"]),
        textcoords="offset points",
        xytext=(0, 7),
        ha="center",
        fontsize=8,
    )

plt.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(score_vingtile_plot_file, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

## Native score risk classes

The score classes are built by grouping the 20 score vingtiles:

- Class 1: vingtiles 1, 2 and 3;
- Class 2: vingtiles 4, 5 and 6;
- Class 3: vingtiles 7 and 8;
- Class 4: vingtiles 9, 10, 11 and 12;
- Class 5: vingtiles 13, 14, 15, 16 and 17;
- Class 6: vingtiles 18, 19 and 20.

Class 1 contains the lowest scores and therefore the riskiest counterparties.  
Class 6 contains the highest scores and therefore the least risky counterparties.
The variable `rating_expert` is the operational score-grid class assigned to each counterparty.

```{python}
score_class_table, base_final_with_score_class = build_score_class_table(
    base_final_with_score=base_final_with_score,
    target="def",
    score_col="score_total",
    n_bins=20,
    min_population_share=0.01,
    min_default_count=500,
)

score_class_table
```

```{python}
score_class_plot_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "default_rate_by_score_class.png"
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(11, 5))

ax.bar(
    score_class_table["score_class"],
    score_class_table["default_rate"],
    color="#D9D9D9",
    edgecolor="#8C8C8C",
    alpha=0.75,
)
ax.plot(
    score_class_table["score_class"],
    score_class_table["default_rate"],
    color="#9D1712",
    marker="o",
    linewidth=2.2,
)

ax.set_title("Default rate by score risk class")
ax.set_xlabel("Score interval, from highest risk to lowest risk")
ax.set_ylabel("Default rate")
ax.set_xticks(score_class_table["score_class"])
ax.set_xticklabels(
    score_class_table["score_interval"],
    rotation=25,
    ha="right",
)
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(PercentFormatter(xmax=1, decimals=0))
ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.3)

for _, row in score_class_table.iterrows():
    ax.annotate(
        f"{row['default_rate']:.1%}",
        (row["score_class"], row["default_rate"]),
        textcoords="offset points",
        xytext=(0, 7),
        ha="center",
        fontsize=9,
    )

plt.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(score_class_plot_file, dpi=200, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

## Score risk class summary table

```{python}
risk_class_summary_table = pd.DataFrame({
    "#": score_class_table["score_class"].astype(int),
    "Score interval": score_class_table["score_interval"],
    "Risk label": score_class_table["risk_label"],
    "Population share": score_class_table["population_share"],
    "Default rate": score_class_table["default_rate"],
    "Relative gap": score_class_table["relative_gap_vs_previous"],
})

risk_class_summary_table
```

```{python}
risk_class_summary_table_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_risk_classes_summary_table_clean.png"
)

display_table = risk_class_summary_table.copy()
display_table["Population share"] = display_table["Population share"].map(
    lambda value: f"{value:.2%}"
)
display_table["Default rate"] = display_table["Default rate"].map(
    lambda value: f"{value:.2%}"
)
display_table["Relative gap"] = display_table["Relative gap"].map(
    lambda value: "-" if pd.isna(value) else f"{value:.2%}"
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(13, 4.6))
ax.axis("off")

mat_table = ax.table(
    cellText=display_table.values,
    colLabels=display_table.columns,
    colWidths=[0.07, 0.25, 0.22, 0.16, 0.18, 0.16],
    cellLoc="center",
    loc="center",
)
mat_table.auto_set_font_size(False)
mat_table.set_fontsize(13)
mat_table.scale(1, 2.25)

for (row, col), cell in mat_table.get_celld().items():
    cell.set_edgecolor("#FFFFFF")
    cell.set_linewidth(1.2)

    if row == 0:
        cell.set_facecolor("#9D1712")
        cell.get_text().set_color("#FFFFFF")
        cell.get_text().set_fontweight("bold")
    elif col == 0:
        cell.set_facecolor("#7F0D09")
        cell.get_text().set_color("#FFFFFF")
        cell.get_text().set_fontweight("bold")
    else:
        cell.set_facecolor("#E9E9E9" if row % 2 == 1 else "#C7C7C7")
        cell.get_text().set_color("#222222")

plt.tight_layout(pad=0.2)
fig.savefig(risk_class_summary_table_file, dpi=220, bbox_inches="tight")
plt.show()
```

## Score class stability over time

Risk class validation requires checking both dimensions over time:

- risk stability: default rate by year and score class;
- volume stability: population share by year and score class.

```{python}
(
    score_class_stability_by_year,
    score_class_stability_summary,
    score_class_risk_pivot,
    score_class_volume_pivot,
) = build_score_class_stability_tables(
    base_final_with_score_class=base_final_with_score_class,
    target="def",
    period_col="year",
    class_col="score_class",
)

score_class_stability_summary
```

```{python}
score_class_risk_stability_plot_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_class_risk_stability_by_year.png"
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

for score_class, class_df in score_class_stability_by_year.groupby(
    "score_class"
):
    ax.plot(
        class_df["year"],
        class_df["default_rate"],
        marker="o",
        linewidth=2,
        label=f"Class {score_class}",
    )

ax.set_title("Risk stability: default rate by year and score class")
ax.set_xlabel("Year")
ax.set_ylabel("Default rate")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(PercentFormatter(xmax=1, decimals=0))
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
ax.legend(ncol=3)

plt.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(
    score_class_risk_stability_plot_file,
    dpi=200,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.show()
```

```{python}
score_class_volume_stability_plot_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_class_volume_stability_by_year.png"
)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 5))

for score_class, class_df in score_class_stability_by_year.groupby(
    "score_class"
):
    ax.plot(
        class_df["year"],
        class_df["volume_share"],
        marker="o",
        linewidth=2,
        label=f"Class {score_class}",
    )

ax.set_title("Volume stability: population share by year and score class")
ax.set_xlabel("Year")
ax.set_ylabel("Population share")
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(PercentFormatter(xmax=1, decimals=0))
ax.grid(alpha=0.3)
ax.legend(ncol=3)

plt.tight_layout()
fig.savefig(
    score_class_volume_stability_plot_file,
    dpi=200,
    bbox_inches="tight",
)
plt.show()
```

## Export scoring outputs

```{python}
score_output_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_computation_outputs.xlsx"
)
base_final_with_score_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "base_final_with_score.csv"
)
default_rate_by_vingtile_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "default_rate_by_score_vingtile.csv"
)
score_class_table_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_risk_classes.csv"
)
base_final_with_score_class_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "base_final_with_score_class.csv"
)
risk_class_summary_csv_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_risk_classes_summary_table.csv"
)
score_class_stability_by_year_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_class_stability_by_year.csv"
)
score_class_stability_summary_file = (
    Path(data_output_path)
    / "model_selection"
    / "score_class_stability_summary.csv"
)

base_final_with_score.to_csv(base_final_with_score_file, index=False)
default_rate_by_vingtile.to_csv(default_rate_by_vingtile_file, index=False)
score_class_table.to_csv(score_class_table_file, index=False)
base_final_with_score_class.to_csv(
    base_final_with_score_class_file,
    index=False,
)
risk_class_summary_table.to_csv(risk_class_summary_csv_file, index=False)
score_class_stability_by_year.to_csv(
    score_class_stability_by_year_file,
    index=False,
)
score_class_stability_summary.to_csv(
    score_class_stability_summary_file,
    index=False,
)

with pd.ExcelWriter(score_output_file, engine="openpyxl") as writer:
    modality_score_table.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="modality_scores",
        index=False
    )
    variable_contributions.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="variable_contributions",
        index=False
    )
    conditional_density.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="conditional_density",
        index=False
    )
    base_final_with_score.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="base_final_with_score",
        index=False
    )
    default_rate_by_vingtile.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="default_rate_vingtile",
        index=False
    )
    score_class_table.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="score_risk_classes",
        index=False
    )
    base_final_with_score_class.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="base_score_classes",
        index=False
    )
    risk_class_summary_table.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="risk_class_summary",
        index=False
    )
    score_class_stability_by_year.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="class_stability_year",
        index=False
    )
    score_class_stability_summary.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="class_stability_summary",
        index=False
    )
    score_class_risk_pivot.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="risk_stability_pivot",
        index=False
    )
    score_class_volume_pivot.to_excel(
        writer,
        sheet_name="volume_stability_pivot",
        index=False
    )
    for dataset_name, scored_df in scored_datasets.items():
        scored_df.to_excel(
            writer,
            sheet_name=f"scored_{dataset_name.lower()}",
            index=False
        )

(
    score_output_file,
    base_final_with_score_file,
    default_rate_by_vingtile_file,
    score_class_table_file,
    base_final_with_score_class_file,
    risk_class_summary_csv_file,
    score_class_stability_by_year_file,
    score_class_stability_summary_file,
)
```
